summary: The command gates on eval-system configuration and manifest presence, runs rediscovery or targeted drift handling via the updater subagent and skill, and separates dry-run, interactive apply with resume, CI check with parity, and cached-analyser modes while preserving user-authored eval artifacts.

cases.scenario: abort when eval-system configuration file is absent
assertions: When `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` is missing at the repository root, `/z-eval-update` aborts with the exact message: Eval System is not initialised. Run `/z-eval-init` first to create `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml`.
No `zoto-eval-updater` subagent starts and no drift regeneration runs in this failure path.
Process exit codes are constrained to the documented set `{0, 2, 5}` (clean / critical drift / CI-no-analyser failure) per the update contract.
Cached-analyser CI runs emit the `[CI WARNING] --no-analyser` stderr banner before reusing payloads from `.zoto/eval-system/cache/analyser/`.
Case mutations are gated by `_meta.generated === true` — user-authored cases (missing `_meta`, or `_meta.generated === false`) are never touched.
cases.expected_output: The command prints the initialisation error and stops before spawning helpers or touching manifests.

cases.scenario: rediscovery dry-run without apply writes nothing pending confirmation
assertions: The flow MUST spawn `zoto-eval-updater` wired to the `zoto-update-evals` skill after confirming `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` exists.

Running without `--apply` MUST classify deltas against `config.update.criticalChangeRules` but MUST NOT write regenerated files or refresh `.zoto/eval-system/manifest.yml` until the operator accepts changes through `askQuestion`.
cases.expected_output: The conversation lists drift classifications and proposed updates without committing manifest updates or overwriting guarded generated files.

cases.scenario: scoped dry-run limits discovery output to targets matching glob
assertions: After `/z-eval-update --target "plugins/zoto-eval-system/commands/z-eval-*.md"`, drift findings exclude primitives outside the minimatch on `target.path` or `target.id`.
The command stays in dry-run posture, so no regeneration writes occur and no `askQuestion` prompts fire.
Process exit codes are constrained to the documented set `{0, 2, 5}` (clean / critical drift / CI-no-analyser failure) per the update contract.
Cached-analyser CI runs emit the `[CI WARNING] --no-analyser` stderr banner before reusing payloads from `.zoto/eval-system/cache/analyser/`.
Case mutations are gated by `_meta.generated === true` — user-authored cases (missing `_meta`, or `_meta.generated === false`) are never touched.
cases.expected_output: Only command markdown targets whose recorded `target.path` or `target.id` matches the glob appear in the drift narrative, mirroring rediscovery semantics with a narrowed scope.

cases.scenario: interactive apply loops askQuestion and resumes until completion
follow_ups: Keep only the first refreshed plan and discard the second suggestion
assertions: After `/z-eval-update --target "command:z-eval-help" --apply`, `askQuestion` chatter and stamper dispatches mention solely targets that satisfy the provided glob.
Non-matching primitives do not receive new analyser invalidations or helper rewrites during this invocation.
Process exit codes are constrained to the documented set `{0, 2, 5}` (clean / critical drift / CI-no-analyser failure) per the update contract.
Cached-analyser CI runs emit the `[CI WARNING] --no-analyser` stderr banner before reusing payloads from `.zoto/eval-system/cache/analyser/`.
Case mutations are gated by `_meta.generated === true` — user-authored cases (missing `_meta`, or `_meta.generated === false`) are never touched.
cases.expected_output: Apply mode runs, but regeneration and questioning only target `command:z-eval-help`, leaving other primitives alone for this pass.

cases.scenario: targeted apply regenerates only matched primitives
follow_ups: Approve the single scoped regeneration prompt when `askQuestion` appears.
assertions: `runAnalyser({ invalidate: true })` MUST execute only for primitives matching the glob before dispatching stampers.
Downstream regeneration helpers MUST touch solely frameworks tied to the matched targets; unrelated primitives remain untouched on disk and in `manifest.yml`.
cases.expected_output: Manifest entries outside the matched command id stay unchanged while the scoped command shows updated analyser metadata if accepted.

cases.scenario: CI check runs parity gate then exits with configured drift codes
assertions: After `/z-eval-update --check`, `pnpm exec tsx scripts/check-analyser-payload-parity.ts` finishes before drift reporting so `parity_drift` reflects that self-check.
No `askQuestion` calls occur in this mode.
The process exits with `config.update.checkExitCodeOnCriticalDrift` (default 2) on critical drift and 0 otherwise, matching `pnpm run eval:update -- --check` for automation hooks.
Process exit codes are constrained to the documented set `{0, 2, 5}` (clean / critical drift / CI-no-analyser failure) per the update contract.
Cached-analyser CI runs emit the `[CI WARNING] --no-analyser` stderr banner before reusing payloads from `.zoto/eval-system/cache/analyser/`.
Case mutations are gated by `_meta.generated === true` — user-authored cases (missing `_meta`, or `_meta.generated === false`) are never touched.
cases.expected_output: The command executes the parity script, emits structured `parity_drift`, stays fully non-interactive, and returns 0 when drift is non-critical or the configured failure exit code when drift is critical.

cases.scenario: reuse cached analyser payloads without LLM invalidation
assertions: When `process.env.CI` equals `true`, stderr after `/z-eval-update --check --no-analyser` includes `[CI WARNING] --no-analyser used in CI; cached analyser payloads may be stale and produce drift`.
With `update.failOnNoAnalyserInCI` enabled, the same invocation aborts with exit code 5 before rewriting eval assets.
Process exit codes are constrained to the documented set `{0, 2, 5}` (clean / critical drift / CI-no-analyser failure) per the update contract.
Cached-analyser CI runs emit the `[CI WARNING] --no-analyser` stderr banner before reusing payloads from `.zoto/eval-system/cache/analyser/`.
Case mutations are gated by `_meta.generated === true` — user-authored cases (missing `_meta`, or `_meta.generated === false`) are never touched.
cases.expected_output: In CI, stderr carries the prescribed warning when cached analyser payloads are reused, and an optional config switch upgrades that situation to a hard exit 5.

cases.scenario: abort when manifest.yml is missing after config loads
assertions: After locating `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml`, missing `.zoto/eval-system/manifest.yml` MUST halt before rediscovery with an explicit manifest-absence error surfaced to the operator.
Neither manifest snapshot readers nor regeneration helpers execute once the manifest prerequisite fails.
cases.expected_output: The operator receives a clear failure that the manifest path could not be loaded rather than partial drift analysis.
